content.full_text:
Re
Farn Pourrcel PnacrrcEs CoruvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
March 14,2005
The Honorable Thomas M. Kasten
Mayor, City of Hillsborough
1320 Buckingham Way
Hillsborough, CA 94010
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.I-05'037
Dear Mayor Kasten:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding your reporting obligations
on the statement of economic interests under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").I Because
your questions are general in nature, we are treating your letter as a request for informal
assistance.2
QUESTION
As a city council member, are you required to report an investment in Dell, Inc., which
conducts its business via the internet only, on your Form 700, Statement of Economic Interests?
CONCLUSION
Yes, you must report your investment in Dell, Inc. on your Form 700 if your ownership
interest is $2,000 or more.
FACTS
You are a member of the city council and serving as mayor for the City of Hillsborough.
The city does not have commercial zoningor places of business; it is strictly a residential
community consisting of single family homes and four public schools, two private schools, and
two private clubs.
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-
18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does flot provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed).


Our File No. I-05-037
page2
ANALYSIS
The Act requires certain public officials, such as city council mernbers, to disclose all
sources of incomg all interests in real property, and all investments in business entities.
(Sections 87200-87210.) For purposes of disclosing investments, interests of $2,000 or more are
reportable and can be stocks, rights, warrants, options, debt instruments, and anypartnership .;
ownership interest owned directly, indirectly orbeneficially by the pubiic officiai o, u .rr"rrib",
of his or her immediate family. The investment is reportatte iittre blusiness entity or any parent,
subsidiary, or otherwise related business entity has an interest in real property in an official,s
jurisdiction, does business or plans to do business within the jurisdiction, or has done business
within the jurisdiction at any time during the two years priorio the time any statement is required
under the Act. (Section 52034.)
While the Act does not define the term "doing business in the jurisdiction,,, the
Comrnission interpreted the phrase's meaning in its opinion, In re Baty (lg7g) S fppC Ops. 10,
and concluded that 1n entity is "doing business in theJurisdiction,, if tleentity,,has businLss
contacts in the jurisdiction." Regulation 18230 fuither defines the phrase by stating:
'.A person is 'doing business in the jurisdiction' if that person has
business contacts on a regular or substantial basis with a person
who maintains a physical presence in the jurisdiction of a public
official. 'Business contacts' include, but are not limited t;,
manufacturing, distributing, selring, purchasing, or providing
services or goods. 'Business contacts' do not include markelting
via the Internet, terephone, television, radio, or printed media.,,
Thus, simply because a business entity has a website that is accessible to those in Hillsborough
with intemet accessibility is not enough to qualifu the business entity as ..doing business in the
jurisdiction." However, if a company does business on a regular ani substantial basis with a
resident or other persons with a physical presence in Hillsborough, it would be enough to trigger
"doing business in the jurisdiction.,'
Dell, Inc' obviously does business in Hillsborough bv selling its products and services to
residents and others located in the city. In fact, the city of HittsUorlugh itself may purchase
Dell, Inc. products or seryices
You also ask whether other businesses, such as The Gap, Toyota, and United Airlines,
which do not have a store in Hillsborough, and may or *uy ooi sell products or services via the
internet, would be reportable investments. It is reasonable to assume that businesses the size of
these entities are doing business in the jurisdiction by way of sales to residents and other persons
with a physical presence in Hillsborough. However,the bommission will not respond to
hypothetical questions. (Regulation 1 8329(cX4).)


Our File No. I-05-037
Page 3
Whether other business entities are doing business in your jurisdiction would depend
upon the specific business. You must "verify," under penalty of perjury, that you have used
reasonable diligence in preparing your statement and that to the best of your knowledge the
information on the statement is true and complete. The description provided above should help
with that determination. If you are unsure whether a business entity is doing business within
your jurisdiction, you may want to make a written inquiry of the company. (Knuclson Advice
Letter, No. A-99-067.)
If you have any other questions regarding this matter,please contact me at (916) 322-
5660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General C
S. Moen, PhD
Political Reform Consultant II
Technical Assistance Division

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: As a city council member, are you required to report an investment in Dell, Inc., which
conducts its business via the internet only, on your Form 700, Statement of Economic Interests?

CONCLUSION: Yes, you must report your investment in Dell, Inc. on your Form 700 if your ownership
interest is $2,000 or more.
